text:
REGIME — Defensive rotation day; small caps outperformed, staples lagged, breadth narrow.

INDICES — VIX 25.8, elevated but off 52-week high of 52.3. Best to worst: IWM +0.22%, IJH +0.13%, SPY +0.04%, QQQ +0.02%, DIA -0.19%. Small caps beat large caps across the board. Gold surged +0.97% as the dollar fell -0.29%, the sharpest cross-asset divergence of the session.

SECTORS — XLE +0.80%, XLK +0.48%, XLU +0.22%, XLV +0.20%, XLC +0.05%, XLF +0.00%, XLRE -0.10%, XLI -0.20%, XLB -0.28%, XLY -1.16%, XLP -1.69%. Top industries: WGMI +2.37% and XES +2.08%; bottom industries: XHB -2.23% and MSOS -8.56%.

THE 7s — Buildout +1.55%, Semi +1.43%, Old Metals +1.14%, Fab +0.79%, Energy +0.71%, Health +0.53%, Power +0.36%, Cloud +0.33%, Bank +0.23%, Industrial +0.07%, New Staples -0.06%, Insurance -0.10%, Freight -0.18%, Software -0.25%, Medtech -0.25%, Toll Road -0.35%, Defense -1.02%, New Metals -1.03%, Old Staples -1.90%, Housing -1.91%. Strongest ticker in Buildout 7 leads; Housing 7's weakest dragged the bottom.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2, Neutral. Top movers: UNH +9.37% (Health 7, 2.5x vol spike), WGMI +2.37% (Industries), MSOS -8.56% (Industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +7.52%, worst Software 7 -9.85%; best basket Energy 7 +9.02%, worst basket Software 7 -9.85%. UNH's +9.37% surge on 2.5x average volume likely reflects short-covering or a sector-specific catalyst reversing prior healthcare pressure.

WATCH — UNH +9.37%: 2.5x vol spike on a single-day 9%+ move warrants confirmation — gap fill or exhaustion tomorrow. XLP -1.69%: worst sector on a defensive day is a red flag; 20d momentum -4.95% deteriorating. MSOS -8.56%: extreme outlier drop deserves a structural look for follow-through selling.
